creator: ejpiper308@gmail.com
text:
Hi,
I found that FreeBSD is using some codes of tcpdump,
and discovered that there could be some risk of buffer over-read in those codes.

tcpdump initially patched CVE-2017-13026
(https://github.com/the-tcpdump-group/tcpdump/commit/b20e1639dbac84b3fcb393858521c13ad47a9d70),

and after that, they additionally patched the code to do more checks for running past the TLV and subTLV length.
(https://github.com/the-tcpdump-group/tcpdump/commit/0fa0692de376611b29bac4047361c924d21372eb).

It seems that latest version of FreeBSD already applied the CVE-2017-13026 patch,
but its additional supplementary patch hasn't been applied.

I think it would be safer to prevent the risk by applying their supplementary patch.
Thank you.
